title: Refresher LPN Theory
catalogDescription: This course is designed to provide a comprehensive review and update of current practical nursing concepts for LPNs re-entering the profession. Course content covers the nursing process, pharmacology, and the evolving role of the practical nurse within today's healthcare system. Topics include legal expectations, the Washington Nurse Practice Act, and practical nursing ethics. The course will integrate physical, biological, and social sciences to enhance understanding of nursing care across diverse cultural, ethnic, and socioeconomic backgrounds. Participants will build on their previous nursing knowledge to gain a comprehensive understanding of nursing fundamentals, medical-surgical care, and mental health nursing.

title: Refresher LPN Laboratory
catalogDescription: Designed to help LPNs re-entering the profession acquire and practice the essential nursing skills needed before beginning clinical practice. Participants will engage in structured skill-building exercises covering foundational nursing procedures, including patient assessment, vital signs, medication administration, wound care, and infection control. This lab-based course emphasizes the development of technical proficiency, safety, and accuracy in nursing interventions. Participants will demonstrate effective communication and teamwork skills necessary for delivering patient-centered care. The course aims to build confidence and competence in practical nursing skills that will be applied in clinical settings. This is a pass/no credit course.

title: Refresher LPN Clinical
catalogDescription: This course provides practical nursing students with supervised clinical practice in real-world healthcare settings. Participants will gain hands-on experience in clinical decision-making, patient care, and the professional roles of practical nurses, with a focus on medical-surgical, mental health, and geriatric care. Clinical experiences and simulations will reflect care across the lifespan and emphasize cultural competence, patient safety, and collaboration within interdisciplinary teams. By the end of the course, participants will be prepared to transition into the practical nursing workforce in Washington State, understanding their professional identity and scope of practice. This is a pass/no credit course.
